tool.name: wordfinder
tool.summary: report maximal exact shared regions between one query and multiple targets

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `wordfinder` extends the exact-region model of `wordmatch` to one query sequence against a target set. Epithema v1 preserves target-record order and reports maximal exact ungapped regions in a stable table.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts exactly one local query record, one local target set, and an optional `--word-size` threshold. Each target record must resolve to a compatible molecule class relative to the query.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The output is a stable table with one row per maximal exact shared region across the target set. Each row reports query and target identifiers, 1-based inclusive spans in both sequences, match length, and exact matched text.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema wordfinder`. Validation currently covers one committed query fixture and one committed two-record target set through the Rust tool and service layers.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release is an exact multi-target region reporter. It does not implement approximate neighborhoods, heuristic large-sequence acceleration, or graphical word-space output.

artifacts.label: Wordfinder query FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed one-record query FASTA fixture used for multi-target exact-region reporting.

artifacts.label: Wordfinder targets FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed two-record target FASTA fixture whose first record shares one exact region with the query.

examples.title: Report one exact shared region across a target set
examples.description: Runs `wordfinder` against a committed one-record query FASTA fixture and a committed two-record target set.

expected_outputs.label: Multi-target exact shared-region table
expected_outputs.description: One row is reported for the target record `wf_target_a`, with no hit rows for the second target.
